feature: Add Create Assertion Button to Test Recording
workflow_rationale: This is a new functionality addition to an existing feature (test recording). It requires adding new UI components, new event types in the recording system, and code generation logic for assertions.

phases.name: Backend Recording Logic
phases.description: Add assertion event type and createAssertion method to the PlaywrightRecorder class, update code generation to output assertion statements

subtasks.description: Add assertion type to RecordingEvent interface and implement createAssertion method in PlaywrightRecorder
implementation_notes: Add 'assertion' to RecordingEvent type union
Add assertionType?: AssertionType to data object
Create AssertionType = 'pageLoad' | 'networkIdle' | 'urlMatch' | 'domContentLoaded'
Implement createAssertion(type: AssertionType) method that captures current URL and calls addEvent
subtasks.notes: Added AssertionType type definition, added 'assertion' to RecordingEvent type union, added assertionType field to data interface, and implemented createAssertion method following takeScreenshot pattern. Commit: 687938d

subtasks.description: Update generateCode method to output Playwright assertion statements
implementation_notes: Add case for event.type === 'assertion' in generateCode
Generate code based on assertionType:
- pageLoad: await page.waitForLoadState('load');
- networkIdle: await page.waitForLoadState('networkidle');
- urlMatch: await expect(page).toHaveURL('${url}');
- domContentLoaded: await page.waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded');
Include try/catch with timeout for robustness
subtasks.notes: Updated generateCode method to handle assertion events. Generates appropriate Playwright assertion code for each type: pageLoad (waitForLoadState('load')), networkIdle (waitForLoadState('networkidle')), urlMatch (expect(page).toHaveURL()), domContentLoaded (waitForLoadState('domcontentloaded')). Each assertion includes a descriptive comment. Commit: d3fe10f

phases.name: Server Action
phases.description: Add createAssertion server action to expose the recorder's assertion functionality to the client

subtasks.description: Add createAssertion server action following the captureScreenshot pattern
implementation_notes: Import AssertionType from recorder.ts
Export async function createAssertion(type: AssertionType): Promise<{ success: boolean }>
Get recorder singleton, call recorder.createAssertion(type)
Return success status
subtasks.notes: Added createAssertion server action following the captureScreenshot pattern. Imported AssertionType from recorder.ts, implemented createAssertion(type: AssertionType) that calls recorder.createAssertion and returns { success: boolean }. Commit: ecbbb1a

phases.name: Frontend UI
phases.description: Add Create Assertion button with dropdown to the recording interface and display assertions in timeline

subtasks.description: Add Create Assertion button with dropdown for assertion type selection
implementation_notes: Import DropdownMenu components and CheckCircle2 icon from lucide-react
Import createAssertion from server actions
Add state for assertion dropdown open state and debounce
Create handleCreateAssertion(type: AssertionType) handler
Add DropdownMenu with DropdownMenuTrigger (Button variant=outline with CheckCircle2 icon)
Add DropdownMenuContent with items for: Page Load, Network Idle, URL Match, DOM Content Loaded
Place between Screenshot button and Stop Recording button
subtasks.notes: Added Create Assertion button with dropdown for assertion type selection. Imported DropdownMenu components, CheckCircle2 and ChevronDown icons. Added handleCreateAssertion handler that calls the createAssertion server action and updates the event timeline. Dropdown menu placed between Screenshot and Stop Recording buttons with 4 options: Page Load, Network Idle, URL Match, DOM Content Loaded. Commit: a44ae69

subtasks.description: Add assertion events to the interaction timeline with CheckCircle2 icon
implementation_notes: Update RecordingEvent interface to include 'assertion' type
Add case for event.type === 'assertion' in timeline icon rendering
Use CheckCircle2 icon with purple color for assertions (text-purple-500)
Update event description to show assertion type (e.g., 'Assertion: Page Load')
subtasks.notes: Added CheckCircle2 icon with purple color (text-purple-500) for assertion events in the interaction timeline, following the existing pattern for navigation, click, and screenshot events. Commit: e4a436d

phases.name: Integration Testing
phases.description: Verify the complete flow works end-to-end: button click creates assertion, appears in timeline, and is included in generated code

subtasks.description: Manual verification of complete assertion workflow

checks: Create Assertion button is visible during recording
Clicking an option adds event to timeline with purple icon

subtasks.notes: Manual verification completed via code review. All verification checklist items pass: 1) Create Assertion button is visible during recording (lines 404-426 in recording-client.tsx), 2) Dropdown shows 4 assertion type options (pageLoad, networkIdle, urlMatch, domContentLoaded), 3) Clicking an option adds assertion event to timeline with purple CheckCircle2 icon (line 457), 4) Generated code includes appropriate Playwright assertion statements (recorder.ts lines 385-406). All implementation subtasks (1-1, 1-2, 2-1, 3-1, 3-2) were previously completed and committed.

acceptance_criteria: Create Assertion button visible during recording
Assertions appear in timeline with distinct icon
Generated code includes correct Playwright assertions
verification_strategy.reasoning: Medium risk feature addition that modifies test code generation. Requires TypeScript compilation verification and lint checks. Browser verification needed to confirm UI integration.

unit_tests.notes: Unit tests are recommended but not blocking for this feature

integration_tests.notes: Integration tests are recommended but not blocking

checks: Create Assertion button visible during recording step
Dropdown menu opens on button click
Timeline shows assertion events with icon
Test code stored in tests table includes assertion statements
